_note: Historical crisis knowledge base for rhyming engine. Each crisis has preconditions (weighted), phases, and trading lessons.

crises.name: 2008 Global Financial Crisis
crises.summary: Subprime mortgage crisis → bank failures → credit freeze → global recession. VIX went from 15 to 80+. Credit spreads exploded. Lehman Brothers collapsed. TARP bailout. The worst financial crisis since the Great Depression.

preconditions.description: HY OAS spread above 500bps and widening
VIX complacent below 18 while credit deteriorates
Multiple bank stress headlines in recent weeks

phases.name: Early Warnings
phases.description: Subprime defaults rise, Bear Stearns hedge funds fail, credit spreads widen slowly

phases.name: Acceleration
phases.description: Bear Stearns collapses, Fannie/Freddie nationalized, Lehman fails, AIG bailout

phases.name: Capitulation
phases.description: S&P hits 666, VIX at 80+, TARP enacted, global coordinated rate cuts

phases.name: Recovery
phases.description: QE begins, markets bottom, slow recovery starts

tradingLessons: Credit spreads are the canary — watch HY OAS above 400bps
VIX complacency during credit deterioration is the classic setup
Bank CDS moves precede equity drops by weeks
Correlation goes to 1 during panic — diversification fails
Policy response (TARP/QE) creates the bottom, not fundamentals

crises.name: 2020 COVID Crash
crises.summary: External pandemic shock → fastest bear market in history (33 days). VIX 14 to 82 in 3 weeks. All correlations went to 1. Fed cut to zero and launched unlimited QE. V-shaped recovery.

preconditions.description: VIX extremely complacent below 15 before shock

phases.name: Denial
phases.description: Markets initially shrug off virus news, minor dips bought

phases.name: Panic
phases.description: Fastest bear market ever, circuit breakers, limit downs, credit freeze

phases.name: Policy Response
phases.description: Fed zero rate + unlimited QE, CARES Act, markets bottom

tradingLessons: External shocks can crash markets in days, not months
All correlations go to 1 — only cash hedges work
The speed of Fed response determines the speed of recovery
VIX below 15 + emerging threat = maximum fragility
V-shaped recovery possible when balance sheets are intact pre-crisis

crises.name: 2000 Dot-Com Bubble Burst
crises.summary: Tech bubble burst after years of speculative excess. NASDAQ dropped 78% over 30 months. Narrow leadership (tech), extreme valuations, IPO frenzy. Recession followed.

preconditions.description: VIX elevated but not crisis — market ignoring risk
Yield curve inverted 12-18 months before crash
Market breadth narrow — few stocks driving index
IPO frenzy, unprofitable companies listing at huge valuations
Fed funds rate elevated, tightening cycle

phases.name: Peak Euphoria
phases.description: NASDAQ hits 5000+, daily IPOs, retail speculation peaks

phases.name: Initial Break
phases.description: First major tech selloff, -30%, brief bounces fail

phases.name: Grinding Bear
phases.description: Slow bleed, false rallies, earnings downgrades cascade

phases.name: Capitulation
phases.description: NASDAQ -78% from peak, Enron/WorldCom frauds, recession confirmed

tradingLessons: Narrow leadership (few stocks driving index) is a late-cycle signal
IPO frenzy with unprofitable companies = peak euphoria
Grinding bear markets last longer than anyone expects (30 months)
False rallies in bear markets are violent but fail
The yield curve inversion 12-18 months before was the warning

crises.name: 1987 Black Monday
crises.summary: Portfolio insurance + program trading + overvaluation → single-day 22% crash. VIX equivalent hit 150+. Market recovered within 2 years. No recession followed.

preconditions.description: VIX low despite rising rates and overvaluation
Yield curve positive but rates rising fast
Fed funds rate high and rising
Weak treasury auctions / bond market stress

phases.name: Pre-Crash Weakness
phases.description: Wednesday-Friday before: market drops 10%, options expiration pressure

phases.name: Black Monday
phases.description: October 19: -22% in single day, circuit breakers didn't exist yet

phases.name: Aftermath
phases.description: Fed injects liquidity, markets stabilize, no contagion to real economy

tradingLessons: Mechanical selling (portfolio insurance) can create cascading crashes
Single-day crashes without credit contagion can recover fast
Rising rates + overvaluation + leverage = fragile setup
Fed liquidity injection is the circuit breaker
Program trading / algorithmic risks amplify moves

crises.name: 2022 Rate Shock
crises.summary: Inflation surged post-COVID, Fed hiked aggressively (0% → 5.25%). Yield curve inverted deeply. Crypto contagion (Luna, FTX). Bond market worst year in decades. SPX -25%, NASDAQ -33%.

preconditions.description: Fed funds rate above 4% and rising fast
Hot inflation print headlines, CPI surprises

phases.name: Inflation Surge
phases.description: CPI prints keep surprising hot, Fed pivots hawkish, markets selloff

phases.name: Aggressive Hiking
phases.description: 75bps hikes, yield curve inverts deeply, no pivot

phases.name: Crypto Contagion
phases.description: Luna collapse, FTX fraud, crypto credit cascade

phases.name: Bear Market Bottom
phases.description: SPX bottoms around 3500, inflation starts cooling, Fed signals slower hikes

tradingLessons: Don't fight the Fed — hiking cycles crush multiples
Deep yield curve inversion is the single best recession predictor
Crypto contagion can bleed into traditional markets via leverage
Bond market selloffs of this magnitude are generational events
Inflation persistence is always underestimated by markets

crises.name: 1998 LTCM/Russia Crisis
crises.summary: Russian debt default → EM contagion → LTCM collapse (25:1 leverage) → credit freeze → near-systemic failure. Fed organized bailout. VIX hit 45. Markets recovered within 6 months.

preconditions.description: Credit spreads widening on EM concerns
Contagion from EM to developed markets

phases.name: EM Unwind
phases.description: Russian default, Asian crisis aftermath, EM bonds collapse

phases.name: LTCM Crisis
phases.description: LTCM losses mount, counterparty exposure revealed, credit freezes

phases.name: Fed Intervention
phases.description: Fed organizes LTCM bailout, cuts rates 3 times, markets stabilize

tradingLessons: Leverage is the killer — 25:1 leaves zero margin for error
EM crises can go systemic if leveraged players are exposed
Counterparty risk is invisible until it's not
Fed intervention creates the floor — but timing is uncertain
Credit freeze is the escalation trigger, not equity drawdown

crises.name: 2011 European Debt Crisis
crises.summary: Greek debt crisis → sovereign downgrades → bank contagion (PIIGS) → credit spreads explode → US downgrade by S&P. VIX hit 48. SPX -19%. Resolved by ECB's 'whatever it takes.'

preconditions.description: Credit spreads widening on sovereign concerns
Contagion from periphery to core Europe

phases.name: Greek Crisis
phases.description: Greek debt restructuring, bailout negotiations, austerity protests

phases.name: Contagion
phases.description: Spain, Italy, Portugal yields spike, bank runs in periphery

phases.name: US Downgrade
phases.description: S&P downgrades US from AAA, VIX spikes to 48

phases.name: ECB Intervention
phases.description: ECB LTRO, Draghi 'whatever it takes', markets stabilize

tradingLessons: Sovereign debt crises are slow-motion but can accelerate suddenly
Bank-sovereign doom loop: banks hold sovereign bonds, sovereigns backstop banks
Central bank intervention is the only thing that breaks contagion
US treasuries rally during non-US crises (flight to safety)
Credit downgrades of sovereigns can trigger forced selling cascades

crises.name: 2015 China Devaluation
crises.summary: PBoC devalued yuan → capital flight fears → EM selloff → VIX spiked to 53 → flash crash Aug 24 (Dow -1000 at open). Resolved by PBoC intervention and Fed delay.

preconditions.description: Fed at zero — limited easing room
Liquidity drying up, flash crash risk

phases.name: Devaluation
phases.description: PBoC devalues yuan, initial shock, mild equity selloff

phases.name: Contagion & Flash Crash
phases.description: Capital flight fears, Aug 24 flash crash, VIX 53, circuit breakers

phases.name: Stabilization
phases.description: PBoC intervenes, Fed delays hike, markets recover gradually

tradingLessons: Currency devaluations by major economies can cascade globally
Flash crashes happen when liquidity evaporates — ETFs disconnect from NAV
VIX below 15 + emerging shock = amplified reaction
Central bank FX intervention sets the floor
Fed postponing rate hikes was the signal that crisis was being taken seriously
